Hey everyone, this is Russell and I'm welcoming you back to the Instant Traffic Hacks program. We're gonna be talking right now about how to double your traffic through banner ads and media buying. So right now, the last video I kind of talked about all the research and how you reverse engineer where traffic's coming from. You see what ads people have, what their landing pages are, what they're offering, all those, those core things you need to know to really create an offer that's gonna convert well. So after you know those things and you've gone back and you kind of built out your funnel and things to model what's working, and you know where the traffic is that you're gonna go after, the next step now is we need to create your banner and we need to go and place your very first order. Now, the way that we do it is different from the way some people do it. A lot of people go directly to ad networks like AdBlade or Clickbooth or Yahoo! Emerson, some of these other ad networks, but the problem is if you don't have a campaign that's proven to convert, you can lose literally tens if not, you know, 20, $30,000 very, very quick. In fact, we've done that more than once as we were trying to learn this whole process. We'd go to some of these ad networks and run our ads and didn't know what was working, what creative, what types of pages that didn't work. All those things we talked about in the last video we didn't know. And so because of that, these ad networks will just keep driving your ad like crazy and you just keep losing money, losing money. And some people hit it, but a lot of people, majority of people don't. And so this way the work is showing you's a lot safer and should give you the ability to test things at a smaller level and find out what's working before you ever go into any of the big ad networks. In fact, for us right now, we very rarely even go in the ad networks. We mostly do this direct media buying kind of the way that I'm going to be showing you. So let me dive into this. So the first thing we'll talk about are your banners. Now, there are different banner sizes, right? There's the long leaderboards, there's side banners, a whole bunch of different ones. But the majority of ours are always 300 by 250. And I didn't know that at first. I used to go and create, you know, there's like five or six different banner ad sizes. So I'd get those created for every single thing we were doing. And it was just costing me tons of money. And I finally realized that the sweet spot is 300 by 250. So we just do those first. And then after we find one that works really awesome, we may go back and build out all the other ones. But the reality is we pretty much just focus on those. Okay. So that's the first thing here is that's the size of banners to be working. Now, next thing I talk about, or I like to figure out what are the banner styles? And so as I've been kind of going through this, I've been kind of keeping track of the different styles. And I've broken it down to kind of 11 styles that I like. And so I'll show you the styles that I try to get done. So one of them is an editorial style where it looks like, basically just looks like it came out of like an article, right? So there's my editorial style. And I have my video style, which I like this one a lot, my video style. Then we got our tabloid style. It looks like it was ripped from the tabloids. So there's that one there. Then we've got our handwriting style that are kind of more, that look like they were kind of sketched out. Which is to grab people really well. You can see those. You can see some niches I'm in by just looking at the samples I'm showing you guys. Here's the cartoon style. Again, with most of these banner, as I show you these, notice the ones that like jump out. Like you saw this on a page, you're like, what in the world is that, right? It kind of jumps out at you. Cartoon style. Here's another cartoon style one. I can look at these kind of all day long. These are fun. Another cartoon style. Now here's more cartoon but symptom based. So what's happening inside of you, kind of pointing to what's wrong, what might be hurting. Then we have survey style. These survey ones work awesome when people think they're clicking on a survey. I think I showed you some of those in the last video as well. These work really good. Mini article style. It's like a little mini article. We got a reverse type. Which is like the white text on the black background. Then we've got our classified ad style. Looks like a little classified ad. Then we've got our PPV pop up style. This is kind of like a video style but this PPV is bigger. Those are the different types of ads I usually try to create when I'm going through the styles of ads. Let me shrink these down. That should give you this idea as you're kind of thinking like what are my banner ads going to look like. You're just one of those 11 styles. What I usually do is I find the one I like the most. I'll do that one first and then we'll build other variations. I'll show you that here in a minute. So the first of the type of banners. The next thing I do is I typically, from my very first banner ad set, I go to $20banners.com. I think their designers are amazing. So what I'll do, let me show you. So I go to $20 banners, log in, and then you can buy banners. I say $20 but they always upsells and upgrades and it ends up costing you a little more than that. So what I would typically do is I would come to $20 banners and I'd pick three or four styles that I like. I'll pay these guys to do three or four styles. Just create the initial ones because again, I think their quality is really, really amazing. But it does end up costing quite a bit of money. Each of these sets costs $100 or so. So you usually get one done. See, there's the PPV style ones. If I can do it the way you guys can see them better. Yeah, that style has got a headline video. Anyway, these guys usually do my first variation of one. And then after that, usually after I've got one kind of design done that's nice, then I'll go and get the rest of them done a lot cheaper. What I usually use to get mine are Freelancer.com. I go post a project on Freelancer. So this one, I posted $30 for how many banners did I want? I wanted like, in fact, I don't know if you guys can see the actual project. Yes, here's the brief. I said I need a 300 by 250 and a 665 by 500 version of each of these banners, two images, each of those headlines, a total of eight banners. So basically eight banners and I'm only paid $30 for it. So that's what I paid this dude. He came in and literally within an hour these were done. So I paid him $30 and then he popped out and you can see all these banners. He did for me really quick there. Okay, we did, we took our winning Facebook ad. As you can tell here, took our winning Facebook ad, used the headline from it, the image from it, and turned it into a banner ad. And right now this is one of the banner ads we're running and we're testing. But this way I can get things done a lot faster and cheaper. And so usually, again, for me, I usually with $20 banners to get the first one done and then over here to Freelancer just get a whole bunch more done very, very quickly and cheaply. Okay, and there are tons of people that would have done it for, you know, there's, I ran this project for maybe 10 minutes and all those people do it. So you can get banners done very, very quick and easy through sites like that. So that's how you create your banners initially. Now again, what I would typically do is I would try to get maybe 10 or so banners done, 300 by 250, and again, each of them a different style. And I'll show you why that's important here in a minute. All right, number two is you're going to do your first initial test. And so what we did is from the stuff we did in the first video, we kind of find out where the sites that your other people's traffic is running on. Now, all we're going to do is we're going to go back directly to those sites and try to buy directly from them. Now, mostly sites, if you scroll to the bottom, we're going to have some kind of links as advertised, webmaster, something like that. And you click on that. And usually they're going to have some kind of media kit. Okay, if other people are buying banner ads on their sites, it means you can buy ads as well. Sometimes they broker them out through a third party, but a lot of times, the majority of times, they're selling ads directly. And so you can go just to those sites, contact them, find out who's in charge of selling ads over there. You might need to call them up on the phone sometimes. So they do a little research, but you can find them. And after you know that, then you just come back and you're going to call them on the phone. It's just kind of similar to the solo ad. If you watch the solo ad videos now, you're going to talk to the list owner, talk to the list broker, and you're going to negotiate. And you're going to buy ads from it. It's very similar in this situation, except for you're buying banner ads as opposed to email ads. Okay, now initially we want to test this small. We don't want to go again to an ad network like a lot of people do and go buy ads on 10 million sites. You want to start this small and you're going to test by, test out just three to five different sites. The reason why it's important not just to do one site, it's kind of similar to solo ads. So if you go and you ran a list and it doesn't work, you may think that either your offer doesn't work, or landing page, or whatever, but it might just be the list. Okay, sometimes, I mean, look at our supplement. I would say probably 90% of the lists work for us, but 10% of the lists don't work. And so if we were to have only bought one of those 10% the very first time, we would have thought what we had was a complete failure. So it's important to have multiple different lists to try out. So depending on your budget, I try at least three to five different sites for your initial test. And then the second thing is you're going to need those 10 banners we talked about because sometimes maybe your landing page works good and your offer works good and the site works good, but your banner ads just not going to work. And you don't know that until you test it. So that's why I like doing 10 different variations, 10 different styles of each of my banner ads. And I can plug it in there and let people, and let kind of the traffic run, and I'll talk about it here in a second, and then after the traffic run, you find out which banners are going to be the best because not all banners are going to work. Maybe one out of 10, maybe two out of 10 are going to work. And the other ones you look at, and they're going to be complete failures. The click-through rate is not high and all the other things. And so you got to get good at just kind of testing, finding out what's going to work. So my goal with this test is to get something to work. After I do this first test, I want to find out which site worked and which banner ad was the one that worked the best. That's kind of the goal of this. What I'm going to do is I'm going to run the banner ads for a 2x conversion value. So what that means, let's say you have a product, let's say it sells for $100. So what a lot of people do is they'll spend $50 on banner ads and they'll shut it down. They'll say, oh, nobody bought. But the reality is you've got to run it longer than that. So what we usually do is about a two-time run value. So if our product sells for $100, I'm going to be willing to spend $200 on one banner ad. That's the key there on one banner ad before I declare it either a winner or a loser. If I spent $200 and I haven't broken even yet, or if I'm losing money, I'm going to stop that banner ad. I'm going to kill that banner ad, pull out the queue, and push to another one. I'll show you on the next slide what we do is we use an ad service. So what will happen is I'll place this code on a site and it's going to rotate through my 10 different banners. And really quickly I'm going to find out which ones are working, which ones aren't working. And like I said, after you've run about two times your conversion value on a banner ad, and if it's not profitable, then you kill it. You kill it and you keep killing them. And you kind of quickly weed out and eventually go into this process. You're hoping to find one or two winners. So that's kind of how the testing process actually works. All right. Now, your very first purchase, this is where a lot of people get kind of intimidated. But it's going to be good. Again, some of the keys here is you want to test small. You want to do 300 by 250 banner ads, and you want to be above the fold. Some places will sell you banner ads that are on the bottom of the site, the spot you can never find. And that's not what you want. You want to be above the fold, right? And so that's very important. Next thing, obviously, you're never going to pay a rate card. That's kind of what they, the pricing they put out there for people who don't negotiate, and they never make any money on their deals. So you got to be a smart marketer and basically you're going to negotiate. Say, look, the typical way that we kind of presented is we say, hey, this is the deal. We don't, you know, we're direct response marketers. And if this works well, then we have an infinite budget to scale it infinitely. If it doesn't work well, then we need to cut it fast. And so what we want to do is we just want to test this. So I need you to give me the best testing price you have just so we can see if it's going to work. Okay. You kind of approach that way. They say, okay, this guy knows he's talking about, I'm not going to screw him over with rate card. I'll give him an actual good price because I love for this to work because if it works, then he'll be buying for me every single week, every single month. And so that's kind of some of the ways that we kind of negotiate that. Okay. Other couple of things is you want even delivery. The first time we did this, I did not know that. And so all of our, like we bought, I don't know, a couple thousand, three or $4,000 with the banner ads. We sent it out there and literally within like, I don't know, three hours, everything was spent and it was done. And we didn't even have time to track it or see what was happening or see what was working or not working. We didn't know if it was the time of day. We didn't know if it was the time of the day of the week. We didn't know. There's so much stuff you don't know. In fact, for example, on Facebook, it's really interesting that like from Monday till Friday during working hours is when, I believe that's the stat. Between our working hours, Monday through Friday is when we get customers that are the cheapest for us to acquire and the best quality customers. And then on weekends, it costs more and we get worse quality customers. Okay. I think part of it is because the people we are typically trying to attract are people that own their own companies. And so from nine to five Monday through Friday is when they're at the office working and trying to figure out how to grow their company. And then the weekends, they're hanging out with their family and their kids where the people that are trying to start a business on the weekends where they're not at work and they're trying to figure out how to start a business. And so for us, our better customer is nine to five Monday through Friday because that's when the business owners are working. And so we found that by not running ads on weekends for our market, it actually did better. Okay. And that's something you, if you don't know now, you're going to learn why you're going through this. If you don't do this even delivery stuff, you could get, you know, 100,000 visitors on Thursday and, you know, 100,000 visitors on a Sunday and it didn't work. And you don't know if that's just because all of your target customers weren't there or maybe they're showing you ads at three in the morning. So all the traffic's coming from third world countries. So there's so many different variables. And so like having even delivery where it's kind of spread out, we like putting a frequency cap on it so that somebody's not seeing the banner at a million times in a row. Right. If otherwise I'm just wasting my money, if someone comes to site every page, they go on to see my ad, they see my ad and I'm paying every single time this, this ad pops up in there. And so obviously I want a frequency cap. So the frequency cap, she means how many times in a 24 hour period time can they see that banner ad. 24 means once every day they can see the banner ad no more than that. Okay. And so those are some of the keys you guys in and, you know, with any kind of advertising, the first time you do is kind of scary and there's a lot of unknowns in your, it's not quite sure about it. But it's literally this, this process after you kind of understand it, it's, it's almost the same as buying a solo ad in an email list. I think for the most part, most of the people that we work with have gotten more and more comfortable with that. Where you go, you find a list or list broker, you buy an ad, you write two or three samples or two or three emails, you email creative, you give it to them, they test, they send it out and they split the, you know, split the send. So a third of it goes to each version of your, of your, of the swipe email that you wrote for them. You test to see what subject line works, what headlines, you know, what body copy and, and from that, you know, after you spend, say $2,500 an ad and you watch, you say, okay, $2,500, how much should I make? Oh crap, I lost a thousand or oh great, I made 10,000 and you just kind of do it and then you test the next one and the next one and that's kind of how you, how you do solo ads. Banner ads are the same kind of thing, you guys. You're just dealing with people, but instead of putting an ad in their email newsletter, you're putting an ad on their website. And typically again, the size, the size banner ad that's working is 300 by 250, it's above the fold. Again, you're not, you know, you try and get the price as low as you can, you want an out clause in case it's not working and those are some of the kind of things to focus on. Okay, now let me show you guys, I'm going to show this in the first video, but I want to show you again. So I showed you guys the samples of, of all these banners that I, that I've liked, right? So you look at these and you kind of get ideas of, of the styles, the styles that are, that are working out there. And so if you look over here for us, here are a whole bunch of banner ads that we have in the testing queue right now. So I got 23 of them, we're testing. So I'm kind of addicted to this obviously. So we have a bunch that we're running. We've got hand sketched ones, we've got, in fact, I think I should go with the bottom, you can see bigger versions. We've got these ones here that look like a video, boom, the reverse image here, the animated ones, hand sketched ones, cartoon ones, hand drawn ones, more medical looking ones, more like, looks like an article, looks like a quiz, reverse type, quiz, quiz, video, reverse type. Another version of video with a more of a red background. One showing like a picture of one of our herbs, cartoon version. See, I just, tons of phones I'm testing out because I have no idea which one's going to work, which one's not going to work. And, and pretty soon after you run these, you're going to find out that like, hey, this one's the winner, maybe this one's the winner. You know, one of these is going to be the winner for you. So after you find out what the winners are, they say, let's say I run all 23 of these. And I find out that most of them are break even, but this one right here, man, this one is my sweet spot. Now I take this winning ad, we know wins, and I go out here and I come back, oops, excuse me, I come back, come back here, so these three to five sites. And I can go back to those three to five sites, and you know, maybe it's like I didn't run yet that I found, and I run just that winning ad on it. And all of a sudden, now that one becomes super profitable because I'm not wasting money on the nine or 10 other banners that aren't converting, we're just focusing on the converting one. Okay. And after you have one that's converting, now you can come back and try to create new ones. Because one thing about banner ads that's interesting is that they kind of, you know, people keep going the same websites over and over again. So they have a lifespan. So after, you know, a couple months, it'll stop working, and you know, create another banner and another banner. So it's kind of this game of you keep making new creatives and putting them out there. And as soon as you get in the hits, boom, you run it, you start running in all the sites across the platforms and keep running and keep running until it starts kind of wearing out and dying. And boom, you get the next one in there, and you run that till it's dead and you do the next one. So that's kind of the process. And it's really, it's really a fun and exciting game after you after you learn how it works. Okay. Now one more one more tool you need to get, you don't have to get it, but it'll make your job a million times easier. You just need an ad server. So some ad servers we've used in the past, like ad shuffle, site scout, Zito, they, these are all ad servers. When ad server is basically, it's a, it's a service that'll, that will, that'll run your banner ads for you. Okay. So what that means is you take those 10 banner ads, you go to ad shuffle or site scout and use their ad server and you'll upload all 10 or 11 of those banners to your thing. Right. And then what they'll do is, is this site will give you a little code and that's the code you're going to give back to this website owner. So the person says, okay, I want to, you know, you agree, okay, we're going to run a test. It's going to be $1,000. We're going to get this, this, this and you negotiate all these, okay, send me your banner ads. You go to your ad shuffle account, you upload all your banner ads and you give them this one piece of code. And then he puts that on their site and then this code will start shuffling through your, your 10 different banner ads. And very quickly it'll show you which ones are winning and losing and that kind of a thing. Okay. So it's pretty cool. So it kind of does all in manual work for you to see how many, it'll show you like this banner ad had this many clicks, this many, this many clicks, this many, this many clicks, this many sales. And so really quickly say, well, this one got the most clicks, but this one got the most sales. And, and again, in real time, it's kind of exciting. You watch that and you'll start seeing boom, boom, boom, boom, boom, boom. There's our winner. And so having an ad server is important. Now they're kind of, I'm not going to lie. For whatever reason, they're so far there hasn't been a company that made that has created software that the ad serving software super easy to use. So they're all kind of a pain, but they all have support people you can use. So if you pick whichever one you pick, just contact the person and I would definitely have them walk you through because they are kind of confusing. They're not super intuitive. Unfortunately, they should be a lot easier. Maybe someday we'll create an ad or an ad server because the process of what they're doing is very simple. For every reason, they make the set up very complicated. So just again, whichever company you decide to go with, contact their support and have them walk you through the set up process of it. Unfortunately, we can't support all the different ad servers because there's a lot of them out there. But these are the ones we've had the most experience with and that we recommend for our, for our customers. Alright, you guys, so that's pretty much the core of the Banner Ad Media Buying. It all comes down to step one. Like I talked about in the first video, it's finding out where's that traffic at, where are those people hanging out, where are they congregating, and then what things are currently working. And you click on all the ads like I showed you and find out what the landing pages are. And after you've kind of figured that out and you see what works, then you reverse engineer and you use that to build out yourselves funneled the right way. Then you create the Banner Ad, you buy the ads directly on the sites and you get it to work there. Then after you've gone through this process, you guys, you know, what we recommend and what we still do is just we buy direct from sites. But after there comes a point where it gets cumbersome to manage 100 or 200 or 300 sites, right? And that's when you can start working with different ad networks. And ad networks are basically they're like a network. They work with all these sites as a whole. And so you can come in and work just with the ad network and then buy directly on the on the actual websites. And long term, that's a better strategy, but short term, it's the worst strategy because short term, you're putting your out of there and you're running it across, you know, seven, three to five sites. You're testing small, you're testing it maybe on, you know, 100 sites or 500 sites or 1000 sites. And you got your, you know, two or three banner ads in each one. And it's just it's very, very hard to find out what's going to work. Okay. And get our goal to get something working. And it's just hard process to get that in an ad network. Whereas buying direct, you can get that a lot quicker, a lot easier. And after you know, hey, the, you know, of the three sites we ran this type of site at work the best on. And then this is the bed, the creative that worked the best. Now you go to that network and say, look, this is the site that worked the best. Let's find ones that are very similar demographics to this. This is the ad that worked the best. Let's model this ad for the other things. Now you have a much better success chance inside of the actual ad network. But for most of you guys, you know, until you're past about, you know, a million to $2 million a year in sales, I would just keep going direct to sites. And at that point, you know, we can, we can, that point is where I would start looking at maybe branching out and focusing more on ad networks. Okay. So that's about it, you guys. What I recommend you do is let's go out there and just, just to find the first three sites you want to buy an ad on and just go through the process, test it out. It'll be scary. It'll be exciting. But after you see it and you kind of cut your teeth on it, you're like, man, that wasn't that bad. It was exciting. I want to do it again. And so that's what I want for all of you guys. All right. So thanks again. And I hope you're enjoying the instant traffic hacks. Each of these hacks can drive a ton of traffic to your business. So make sure you implement each of them one at a time. And we'll go from there. Thanks, everybody.
